conditions: Chronic low back pain (degenerative disc disease)
medications: Acetaminophen as needed for back pain
health.mental_health: Depression worsened after living alone in the off-season and the strain of a long-distance relationship; she does telehealth counseling monthly and has occasional insomnia and low appetite during weeks when relocation plans feel uncertain.
recent_visits: Optometrist follow-up for intraocular pressure check (6 weeks ago, Miles City)
DEXA scan review and osteoporosis medication refill (3 months ago, Billings)
Primary care visit for blood pressure check and fatigue (last month)

finance.debt: Still paying on a used 2019 Subaru Outback loan and carries a small balance on a credit card after multiple trips to Washington to see her partner; she is anxious about qualifying for a new lease if she relocates.
investments: Small traditional IRA (rolled over from an earlier retail job)
Savings account earmarked for relocation costs
recent_transactions: Car payment and rideshare platform service fees
Out-of-pocket payment for glaucoma drops after insurance copay change
Two one-way flights to Spokane to visit her partner
Deposit for a storage unit to begin downsizing
Wire transfer to her adult son to help with an emergency vet bill

relationships: Long-distance partner: Daniel Mercer (70), living near Spokane, Washington; discussing moving in together within the year
Adult son: Samir Haddad (39), living in Helena; relationship is supportive but tense about her moving out of state
Neighbor: Marlene Koenig (62), checks in during winter storms and sometimes rides with Irina to Billings appointments
Former coworker friend: Lila Torres (late 50s), occasional phone calls and advice about Medicare and retirement
social.social_media_activity: Uses Facebook mostly on a private profile: posts landscape photos from eastern Montana, shares grandkid pictures occasionally, and uses Messenger daily; she avoids posting about her relationship publicly to prevent gossip among local riders and neighbors.
private_conversations: Negotiating a move-in timeline with Daniel and whether she would sell her trailer or rent it out
Concerns about intimacy and body image related to osteoporosis and depression medications
Arguments with her son about safety and whether she is being pressured to relocate
Discussions about updating her will and medical power of attorney before moving
Talking with Daniel about his adult daughter’s disapproval of their relationship

schedule.work_hours: Typically 6 days/week, 7–9 hour shifts; she prefers late morning to early evening (around 10:30am–7:00pm) to avoid night driving due to glaucoma.
regular_appointments: Monthly telehealth therapy session (first Wednesday afternoon)
Eye pressure check every 3–4 months (Miles City or Billings depending on availability)
Pharmacy pickup for refills every 30–90 days
Twice-weekly bone-strengthening/low-impact exercise class at the community center (when she can fit it in)
upcoming_events: Weekend visit to Spokane to tour apartments with Daniel (in 3 weeks)
DMV appointment for rideshare vehicle inspection and registration renewal (next month)
Follow-up primary care visit to review blood pressure readings (in 6 weeks)
schedule.daily_routine: Coffee, checks eye drops and pill organizer, drives rideshare runs between small towns and the nearest regional hub, eats a simple packed lunch in the car, calls Daniel most evenings, and does stretching/weight-bearing exercises before bed when her back allows.

professional.company: Primarily Uber and Lyft (drives from a rural base and works the Billings/Miles City corridor when demand is higher)
projects: Tracking mileage and expenses weekly to prepare for taxes and potential relocation
Building a regular rider base for medical trips and airport runs
Researching rideshare requirements and driver demand in the Spokane area
professional.performance_issues: Occasional cancellations after dark due to glare and night-vision discomfort from glaucoma; she worries her acceptance rate will drop and has been more distracted on shift when relationship stress spikes.
workplace_relationships: Loose network of local drivers who share surge times and road condition updates in a private text thread
Tense interactions with one competing driver who accuses her of taking long-distance airport runs
Friendly rapport with a few repeat riders who ask personal questions about her move, which she tries to deflect

hobbies: Genealogy research on her phone and laptop
Cooking small-batch soups and stews to freeze
habits: Uses a weekly pill organizer and sets phone alarms for eye drops
Avoids icy sidewalks and wears traction cleats in winter due to fracture risk
Keeps a go-bag in the car for overnight trips to Spokane
Late-night scrolling and messaging when she feels lonely, sometimes leading to poor sleep
Keeps her relationship mostly secret locally to avoid being judged and to reduce passenger gossip
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives alone in a modest single-wide trailer outside a small town in eastern Montana; she is slowly downsizing and sorting belongings for a possible move to Washington to live with her partner, while debating whether to keep the trailer as a fallback.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Simple, budget-conscious meals; limits caffeine after noon to help sleep, tries to increase calcium/protein for bone health, and often relies on portable snacks (nuts, yogurt, fruit) between rides.
